Topic 1: Failing peace & no damage (Iran)

[172:56]developments, a big development as we finally got an Iranian response to the US proposal

Topic 4: The anti-Israel grift & evolving the message

[191:36]Divergence. There is a split in the Israel critical movement. I think that clearly there

[191:42]is now a contingent of grifters and just all around low IQ people that have glommed onto

[191:49]the Israel issue and are really at this point saying nothing intelligent or useful or insightful

[191:57]As we all know, for a very long time, nobody was able to talk about Israel or the Jews

[192:03]in any kind of honest way or critical way or negative way, because of censorship, because

[192:09]of cancel culture, suppression, debanking, all these things that you're very aware of.

[192:14]And in just the past few years, it's become very acceptable to talk about.

[192:19]And you could say also it's become actually popular.

[192:22]It's trendy to talk about.

[192:24]And I think this has created this perverse incentive.

[192:27]We're now a lot of really unremarkable,

[192:31]unintelligent, uninsightful people

[192:34]are just meeting a market demand.

[192:36]They're just pumping out as much anti-Israel content

[192:40]as the market will consume.

[192:43]And typically, this takes the form of very repetitive, very overly simplified, very base

[192:50]kinds of sentiment.

[192:51]It's people every day saying, fuck Israel, it's the Jews, Israel sucks.

[192:57]We've heard this every day, all day for like three years. And there's a contingent of people

[193:03]that I don't even know that they really even care about the issue, know that much about

[193:07]or even understand the issue on a deep level. But at this point, it has just become something

[193:17]a self-reinforcing loop that a lot of people are stuck on, which is, this is what we tweet,

[193:24]this is what gets the engagement, and so that's what we'll do tomorrow and the day after that,

[193:28]and this is the path to some kind of political change or political victory.

[193:33]And I think we need to step outside of that.

[193:36]I think that that has basically run its course.

[193:39]And I think now there are just simply diminishing returns.

[193:47]Stuff, let's be honest, I think it's on its way out.

[193:52]I think it has become passe.

[193:54]I think people are tired of it.

[193:57]It's become stale and it's become a drag.

[194:00]Now, don't misunderstand what I mean when I say that.

[194:03]I'm not saying it is no longer true, and I'm not saying it's no longer worth talking about.

[194:09]And I'm not saying that it's no longer a central issue, and arguably the most important issue.

[194:14]I'm not saying any of that.

[194:16]But what I am saying is that, like anything, every political tactic has a shelf life.

[194:24]and every fad and every trend has a shelf life

[194:28]before inevitably people become disinterested, apathetic.

[194:32]They're ready for something different.

[194:35]And so what I'm saying is not that we need to stop

[194:38]talking about the issue,

[194:39]but that we need to be dynamic as opposed to static.

[194:44]And so instead of the same very simple message,

[194:49]which never is refined, never is

[194:52]recapitulated in a new or interesting way. Instead of, in other words, every day it's just fuck Israel, fuck Israel, fuck, it's them,
